supported_here: R61 stores one saved successor value gate that runs only on exact positive R60 rows if activated later.
R61 fixes internal and external comparators plus overhead accounting as the only admissible bounded-value question.
R61 preserves a strict reading where exact-but-value-negative outcomes count as negative evidence.
unsupported_here: R61 does not represent an executed comparator/value gate yet.
R61 does not itself prove bounded value.
R61 does not widen into broader compiled-family claims.
disconfirmed_here: The idea that any exact useful-kernel carryover would automatically count as systems success without explicit bounded-value accounting.
